title: Defensive Deception against Subspace-Based AoA Localization under Jamming Attacks
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are increasingly vulnerable to sophisticated localization attacks. For example, an adversary can use directional antennas to exploit subspace-based Angle-of-Arrival (AoA) estimation methods, such as the MUSIC algorithm, to localize sensors. In this paper, we mitigate AoA localization attacks by deceiving the jammer through the strategic placement of fake sensors (decoys). We introduce two mitigation strategies: the first maximizes the entropy of the MUSIC pseudospectrum to induce significant uncertainty and ambiguity in the adversary’s angle estimations; the second jointly minimizes the peaks of the MUSIC spectrum at genuine AoAs while maximizing entropy, thereby masking the true sensor locations and enhancing the overall deception effect. To this end, we develop two solution methods for the combinatorial optimization problem: a Random Grid Search (RGS) algorithm and a Cross-Entropy (CE) optimization framework, providing scalable and effective techniques for exploring the discrete space of fake AoA placements. Finally, numerical simulations validate our approach, demonstrating a significant degradation in adversarial localization accuracy.
